data.description:

data.title: Bitcoin Price Could Rally Toward $93K As Bulls Look To Bitcoin Hyper Presale
data.description:
What to Know: Bitcoin trading in a tight range below resistance often pushes traders toward higher‑beta assets that still track the underlying BTC macro trend. Infrastructure that makes Bitcoin more usable for payments, DeFi, and smart contracts is increasingly seen as a leveraged way to express long‑term BTC conviction. Bitcoin Hyper integrates an SVM‑powered Layer 2 with Bitcoin settlement, targeting low‑latency smart contracts to tackle BTC’s speed and programmability limits. Range-bound markets with upside potential toward levels like $93,000 can create strong narratives for BTC-aligned scaling plays and yield-bearing ecosystems
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin-hyper-presale-as-bitcoin-eyes-93k-range-break/

data.title: Grayscale’s Zcash ETF Bid Triggers ‘GBTC 2.0’ Debate: Crash Or Pump Ahead?
data.description:
Delphi Digital researcher Simon Shockey is arguing that the real story in Zcash is no longer just its price – despite ZEC having one of the most eye-popping rallies of this cycle in recent months. “The most interesting thing about ZEC today is not the price,” he wrote on X. “It’s the fact that a GBTC-style discount dislocation just appeared around ZCSH. ” For Shockey, the Zcash trust setup only makes sense when viewed through the lens of what happened with Grayscale’s Bitcoin product
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/grayscales-zcash-etf-crash-or-pump-ahead/

data.title: In the past 24 hours, CEX saw a net outflow of 3958.9 BTC.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, centralized exchanges (CEXs) saw a net outflow of 3958.9 BTC in the past 24 hours. The top three CEXs with the largest outflows were: Binance (3,248.53 BTC), OKX (1,038.57 BTC), and Gemini (512.68 BTC). Bitfinex, on the other hand, saw an inflow of 717.97 BTC, ranking first in terms of inflows.

data.title: ECB Maintains Inflation Outlook Amid Uncertainty
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the European Central Bank's meeting minutes from November 27 indicate that its assessment of the inflation outlook remains largely unchanged. Some officials believe that the cycle of interest rate cuts has concluded, as the favorable outlook is likely to persist unless risks materialize.

The central bank suggests that adopting a prudent strategy could enhance the chances of maintaining a positive state. Strategically, monetary policy should not be adjusted for moderate and temporary inflation fluctuations around the target but should only be altered if there is a significant deviation from the target expected in the medium term. However, most members acknowledge that the risks surrounding the inflation outlook are two-sided, and the inflation outlook remains more uncertain than before. Overall, the value of waiting for more information remains high.

data.title: Wall Street Institutions Forecast Continued Growth for U.S. Stocks by 2026
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, several Wall Street institutions have released their forecasts for the U.S. stock market in 2026, suggesting further growth potential. The ongoing AI boom is expected to continue reshaping the economy and financial markets.

Deutsche Bank has set a year-end target of 8,000 points for the S&P 500 index in 2026. HSBC has projected a target of 7,500 points for the same year. Morgan Stanley also anticipates a strong year ahead, predicting the index will close at 7,800 points in 2026. The firm's strategist, Mike Wilson, referred to this as a "new bull market," noting in a report last week that the rolling recession ended earlier this year, with policy support and earnings strength expected to persist into next year.

JPMorgan shares a similar outlook, forecasting a benchmark target of 7,500 points for 2026. However, the bank believes that if inflation prospects improve, prompting the Federal Reserve to adopt a more aggressive rate-cutting approach, the index could surpass 8,000 points. Currently, JPMorgan expects the Federal Reserve to implement two more rate cuts before pausing.

data.title: The Gate contract points system has been online for 30 periods, with a cumulative single account earnings exceeding $1,300.
data.description: Official data shows that since the launch of Gate's pioneering contract points system, it has completed 30 airdrop events, covering over 30 popular cryptocurrencies. The cumulative earnings per account have exceeded $1300, with the highest single-event airdrop yielding over $127. The performance is impressive. It is understood that Gate's contract points mechanism is the first of its kind in the industry, creating a virtuous cycle between trading incentives and community rewards, bringing continuous high returns to active users.

data.title: Circle Mints 1.25 Billion USDC Amid Market Activity
data.description: According to Odaily, Lookonchain monitoring has revealed that Circle minted 1.25 billion USDC today. Since the market downturn on October 11, Tether and Circle have collectively minted 17.25 billion stablecoins.
